# v2.2.1 — Enterprise-Scale Cloud Scanning

Scan an entire AWS Organization, Azure tenant, or GCP org hierarchy in a single session. Includes automatic retry on rate limiting, checkpoint/resume for interrupted scans, per-record-type DNS breakdown, and significantly expanded resource coverage across all three cloud providers — categories verified against the Engineering token-allocation spreadsheet.

## Multi-Account Scanning

### AWS Organizations
- **Org-level scanning** — enter management account credentials and a cross-account role name; the tool discovers all child accounts via AWS Organizations and scans them in parallel using per-account AssumeRole
- Management account is detected automatically (no self-assume-role)
- Per-account failures are non-fatal — other accounts continue scanning
- New credential form: "Org Scanning" auth method with org role name field

### Azure Multi-Subscription
- **Auto-discovery** — all tenant subscriptions are discovered and pre-selected automatically after validation
- Subscriptions scan concurrently with per-subscription progress reporting
- Per-subscription failures are non-fatal — other subscriptions continue

### GCP Multi-Project
- **Org-level scanning** — authenticate with an org-level service account; the tool discovers all projects via Resource Manager v3 with recursive folder traversal (breadth-first, handles deeply nested hierarchies)
- New credential form: "Org Scanning" auth method with org ID field

## Retry & Reliability

- **Automatic retry with exponential backoff** — API rate limiting (429) and transient errors (500/502/503/504) trigger automatic retry instead of scan failure, across all three cloud providers
- Retry events are visible in the scan progress stream
- Configurable **concurrency per provider** via Advanced Options in the scan UI (defaults to 5 concurrent accounts/subscriptions/projects)

## Checkpoint / Resume

- **Long scans can be resumed** — if a multi-account scan is interrupted, restarting picks up from where it left off without re-scanning completed accounts, subscriptions, or projects
- Checkpoint files are written automatically after each unit completes

## DNS Record Type Breakdown

Results now show per-record-type DNS counts instead of a single aggregate:

- `DNS Record (A)`, `DNS Record (AAAA)`, `DNS Record (CNAME)`, `DNS Record (MX)`, `DNS Record (TXT)`, `DNS Record (SRV)`, and more
- Applies to AWS Route53, Azure DNS, and GCP Cloud DNS
- CSV and HTML exports include per-type labels

## Expanded Resource Coverage

Resource categories are verified against the Engineering token-allocation spreadsheet. Only resources with a confirmed "Yes" in the Needs Token column are counted.

### AWS — 14 resource types (was 7)

| Resource | Token Category |
|---|---|
| VPC | DDI Objects |
| Subnet | DDI Objects |
| IPAM Pool | DDI Objects |
| VPC CIDR Block | DDI Objects |
| Route53 Resolver Endpoint | DDI Objects |
| EC2 Instance IP | Active IPs |
| Elastic IP | Active IPs |
| EC2 Instance | Managed Assets |
| Load Balancer | Managed Assets |
| Internet Gateway | Managed Assets |
| NAT Gateway | Managed Assets |
| Transit Gateway | Managed Assets |
| VPN Gateway | Managed Assets |
| Customer Gateway | Managed Assets |

> Route Tables and Security Groups are **not counted** — confirmed No/N/A in Engineering spreadsheet.

### Azure — 13 resource types (was 6)

| Resource | Token Category |
|---|---|
| VNet | DDI Objects |
| Subnet | DDI Objects |
| DNS Zone | DDI Objects |
| Public IP | DDI Objects |
| VM IP | Active IPs |
| LB Frontend IP | Active IPs |
| VNet Gateway IP | Active IPs |
| Virtual Machine | Managed Assets |
| Load Balancer | Managed Assets |
| Application Gateway | Managed Assets |
| NAT Gateway | Managed Assets |
| Azure Firewall | Managed Assets |
| VNet Gateway | Managed Assets |
| Virtual Hub | Managed Assets |

> Route Tables are **not counted** — confirmed N/A in Engineering spreadsheet.

### GCP — 9 resource types (was 5)

| Resource | Token Category |
|---|---|
| VPC Network | DDI Objects |
| Subnet | DDI Objects |
| DNS Zone | DDI Objects |
| Internal Range | DDI Objects |
| GKE Cluster CIDR | DDI Objects |
| Secondary Subnet Range | DDI Objects |
| Compute Instance IP | Active IPs |
| Compute Address | Active IPs |
| Compute Instance | Managed Assets |
| Network Load Balancer (Forwarding Rule) | Managed Assets |

> Cloud Routers, VPN Gateways, VPN Tunnels, and Firewalls are **not counted** — no confirmed Yes in Engineering spreadsheet.

## Notes

- GCP GKE cluster counting requires the `container.viewer` role on the service account — degrades gracefully to 0 if not present
- GCP org scanning requires `resourcemanager.projects.list` and `resourcemanager.folders.list` on the org-level service account
- AWS org scanning requires `organizations:ListAccounts` on the management account and a cross-account role with read permissions in each child account
- Existing single-account/subscription/project scanning is unchanged — org mode is opt-in via the new auth method

## Download

| Platform | File |
|----------|------|
| Windows | `uddi-token-calculator_windows_amd64.exe` |
| macOS (Apple Silicon) | `uddi-token-calculator_darwin_arm64` |
| Linux | `uddi-token-calculator_linux_amd64` |
